docs.title: Limify
docs.text:
Limify is a Redis-backed, framework-agnostic rate limiting engine for Python.

It provides a clean architecture core with pluggable adapters, async and sync execution, and atomic Redis Lua algorithms.

Limify is designed for:

  • Backend engineers
  • API platforms
  • Microservices architectures
  • SaaS platforms with multi-tier limits

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Framework-agnostic core
  • Async and sync Redis support
  • Atomic Redis Lua execution
  • Rule-based rate limiting
  • Plan-based rate limiting
  • Multiple identity types
  • Clean architecture design
  • Pluggable algorithms and storage

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from limify import Limify, LimifyConfig

config = LimifyConfig(
    rules=[
        {
            "id": "items",
            "method": "*",
            "path": "/items",
            "rate": "10/minute",
        }
    ]
)

Use the navigation menu to explore the documentation.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Limify follows layered clean architecture.

Application (FastAPI / etc.) ↓ Adapter (Middleware) ↓ Limiter (Orchestrator) ↓ Resolvers ├─ RuleResolver ├─ PlanResolver └─ KeyResolver ↓ Algorithm (Token Bucket) ↓ Storage Adapter (Redis)

docs.title: Design Goals
docs.text:
• Framework independence
  • Deterministic behavior
  • Testability
  • Dependency injection
  • Pluggable algorithms
  • Pluggable storage

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Limify uses LimifyConfig to define rules.

Example:

config = LimifyConfig(
    rules=[
        {
            "id": "items",
            "method": "*",
            "path": "/items",
            "rate": "10/minute",
            "priority": 10,
        }
    ]
)

Higher priority rules override lower priority rules.

docs.title: Identity Resolution
docs.text:
Default identity priority:

  1. user_id
  2. org_id
  3. api_key
  4. client_ip
  5. anonymous

Each identity has an independent rate limit bucket.

docs.title: Plans
docs.text:
Limify supports SaaS-style plans.

Example:

free → 10/minute
pro → 100/minute
enterprise → 1000/minute

If no custom plan provider is used, Limify derives the plan from the rule rate.

docs.title: Rate Format
docs.text:
Rates use the format:

<limit>/<unit>

Examples:

5/second
10/minute
100/hour
1000/day

Example conversion:

10/minute → limit=10, period_seconds=60

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
Rules define rate limits for endpoints.

Example:

{
    "id": "items",
    "method": "*",
    "path": "/items",
    "rate": "10/minute",
    "priority": 10,
}

Supported wildcards:

/items/*
/api/**

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • Redis

docs.title: Install Limify
docs.text: pip install limifyrate

docs.title: Install with FastAPI adapter
docs.text: pip install "limifyrate[fastapi]"

docs.title: Development Installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/pedram-aliniaye-asli/limify.git
cd limify
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Redis Setup
docs.text:
Example Redis instance:

redis-server

Or using Docker:

docker run -p 6379:6379 redis

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Example using FastAPI with async Redis.

import redis.asyncio as redis

from fastapi import FastAPI
from limify import Limify, LimifyConfig
from limify.adapters.fastapi.middleware import LimifyMiddleware
from limify.core.redis_adapter import RedisAsyncAdapter
from limify.core.algorithms.token_bucket import AsyncTokenBucketAlgorithm

redis_client = redis.from_url("redis://localhost:6379")

adapter = RedisAsyncAdapter(redis_client)

algorithm = AsyncTokenBucketAlgorithm(adapter)
await algorithm.initialize()

config = LimifyConfig(
    rules=[
        {
            "id": "default",
            "method": "*",
            "path": "/**",
            "rate": "5/minute",
        }
    ]
)

limify = Limify(
    algorithm=algorithm,
    config=config,
)

app = FastAPI()

app.add_middleware(
    LimifyMiddleware,
    limiter=limify.limiter,
)

docs.title: Custom Algorithm
docs.text: Implement a new algorithm class and use the storage adapter for persistence.

docs.title: Custom Storage
docs.text: Create a storage adapter implementing script loading and execution.

docs.title: Versioning
docs.text:
Limify follows semantic versioning.

Example:

0.1.0 0.1.1 0.2.0

docs.title: Publishing
docs.text:
git tag v0.1.3
git push origin v0.1.3

GitHub Actions publishes the package to PyPI.

docs.title: Algorithms
docs.text: Currently implemented:

docs.title: Token Bucket
docs.text:
The algorithm:

  • refills tokens over time
  • consumes tokens per request
  • rejects requests when tokens are empty

Redis Lua ensures atomic execution.

docs.title: Framework Adapters
docs.text:
Supported framework adapters:

  • FastAPI
  • Starlette

The middleware enforces rate limits and adds headers:

  • X-RateLimit-Limit
  • X-RateLimit-Remaining
  • Retry-After

docs.title: Storage Adapters
docs.text:
Supported storage adapters:

  • RedisAsyncAdapter
  • RedisSyncAdapter

Adapters abstract Redis operations used by algorithms.
